dataset: Breakthrough Listen · Voyager 1 · GBT X-band (single coarse channel, fine res)
note: Cards emitted from a REAL narrowband search on a real BL data file. 'measured' holds the actual numbers; 'signal' is the cosmetic panel mapping.

cards.name: Voyager 1 — recovered carrier
cards.source: Real Breakthrough Listen data · Green Bank Telescope · Voyager 1 (X-band)
cards.why: This is a genuine detection from a real BL data file. A narrowband tone at 8419.297 MHz, drifting at -0.377 Hz/s (SNR ≈ 344). The search recovered 3 narrow tones — the carrier plus transmitter sidebands, exactly the structure of a real spacecraft downlink. It has the exact fingerprint we hunt for — steady, narrow, Doppler-drifting.
cards.story: The twist: it's us. This is Voyager 1's transmitter, 20+ billion km away, recovered from the actual telescope data by the same narrowband search SETI runs on every star. Finding our own probe is how the method is proven — a real technosignature, made by human hands.

cards.name: Voyager 1 field — quiet band
cards.source: Real Breakthrough Listen data · Green Bank Telescope · Voyager 1 (X-band)
cards.why: A real stretch of the same observation with no coherent signal — just the receiver's own noise. Most of the sky is quiet, and confirming a band is clear is a real, publishable result: one more place the galaxy is silent.
cards.story: Same real data file, a different slice of frequency. No narrowband tone rises above the noise here. Ruling a region out isn't a failure — it's the other half of the search.
